contactModal.title: Contact us
contactModal.description: For enterprise partnerships, data licensing, technical support, or plan enquiries, email us:

cookieBanner.title: Cookie settings
cookieBanner.description: We use essential cookies to keep you signed in and secure, and analytics cookies to improve the product. We never use them for ad tracking.

hero.title: A Taiwan market data API built for systems and quant workflows
hero.subhead1: A consistent Taiwan stock data API for AI agents, automation, and quantitative research.
hero.subhead2: Centered on TWSE-listed data, with verified datasets that clearly state their coverage window, sources, and limits.

infra.heading: Taiwan market data infrastructure
infra.subheading: Sourced from official providers and shaped into a stable, queryable, traceable API.

twseFirst.label: Verified baseline
twseFirst.description: Centered on listed-market data, expanding coverage to other markets over time.

lowLatency.label: Built for API & agent workflows
lowLatency.description: Tuned for automated queries and research pipelines, with a continually improved read path.

officialFirst.label: Lineage & data gaps preserved
officialFirst.description: Prioritizes TWSE, TPEx, MOPS, and official public sources — no mixed provenance.

clearBoundary.label: No overclaiming
clearBoundary.description: No claims of full-market, adjusted prices, survivorship-safety, or investment advice.

finalCta.heading: Get started
finalCta.body1: From prices and financials to corporate events, connect to the Taiwan market data API in minutes.
finalCta.body2: After signing up you can choose a plan, create your own API keys, and get data access and usage quota by plan.

aiAgentWorkflow.title: AI agent workflow
aiAgentWorkflow.intro: From task planning to reliable data retrieval — built for AI agent and quant workflows, so data is verifiable, traceable, and programmatically usable.
aiAgentWorkflow.step1Description: The AI agent parses the user's question and plans the required data and metrics.
aiAgentWorkflow.step2Title: Find the right dataset and fields
aiAgentWorkflow.step2Description: Use the data catalog and schema to select the exact dataset and fields.
aiAgentWorkflow.step3Title: Call the API to retrieve data
aiAgentWorkflow.step3Description: Retrieve data through a standardized API; responses include source and lineage.
aiAgentWorkflow.step4Description: Check freshness, source tier, and field completeness to ensure reliability.
aiAgentWorkflow.step5Description: Compute metrics, engineer features, or run analysis to produce traceable conclusions.
aiAgentWorkflow.step6Description: Return results while retaining source, timestamp, and lineage — verifiable and auditable.
aiAgentWorkflow.footnote: Traceable, verifiable, and programmatic — built for agent workflows.

agentWorkflowShowcase.title: Let AI agents read traceable Taiwan stock data directly
agentWorkflowShowcase.description: Wire prices, financials, and disclosures into your agent workflow. A consistent response structure with source_role / lineage keeps the data both readable and verifiable.

agentDocumentsShowcase.title: Give AI agents direct understanding of filings and financial reports
agentDocumentsShowcase.description: Turn material announcements, filings, and financial-statement notes into analyzable text context, so agents don't just read the numbers — they understand what a company actually disclosed.

marketCoverage.heading: Query across TWSE- and TPEx-listed stocks, ETFs, warrants, and more
marketCoverage.description: Covers the Taiwan market's main data themes — prices, financial statements, operating metrics, and corporate events. Supports queries across stocks, time, and multiple datasets, so agents can run analysis and screening in a single data layer. Positioned publicly as a TWSE-first verified baseline; makes no claim to full-market historical coverage.

apiDemo.heading: Clean, direct Taiwan stock market data API
apiDemo.description: Consistent endpoints and response formats to quickly integrate market quotes, financial statements, revenue, and event data into your systems.

sourceOfTruth.heading: A single trusted source of truth
sourceOfTruth.description: Sample data topics currently at the public boundary. The public scope uses a TWSE-first verified baseline; TPEx historical coverage, adjusted prices, and a survivorship-safe universe remain deferred / in-progress.

monthly_revenue.title: Monthly Revenue
monthly_revenue.description: MOPS monthly revenue, YoY / MoM growth tracking

twse_daily_price.title: Daily Prices
twse_daily_price.description: TWSE daily prices and trading-day data (TPEx history deferred)

financial_statements.title: Financial Statements
financial_statements.description: Income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement

technical_indicators.title: Technical Indicators
technical_indicators.description: MA, RSI, MACD and other common technical indicators

valuation_data.title: Valuation Data
valuation_data.description: P/E, P/B, dividend yield and market-cap metrics

capital_flow.title: Chips & Capital Flow
capital_flow.description: Institutional net buy/sell, margin & short, and capital flow

company_events.title: Company Events

market_structure_snapshot.title: Market Structure Snapshot

pricing.title: Pricing

selfServe.instantDesc: Pay online and your API key is issued immediately — no contract, no waiting for a sales callback.
selfServe.cancelDesc: Turn off any monthly plan from your account page — no lock-in, no cancellation fee.
selfServe.selfDesc: Sign up, get a key, watch usage, upgrade — all self-service; reach us only when you need to.

pricing.annualCheckoutNote: Annual checkout is coming soon; to start now, switch to monthly.

pricing.cardRecurringNote: Recurring card billing; cancel anytime from the Customer Portal on the billing page.
pricing.enterpriseNote: Enterprise / custom: standard plans can be paid by card directly; contact us for dedicated quota, invoicing, or an SLA.
pricing.notAdvice: This service provides data APIs, tools, and documentation — not investment advice, stock recommendations, or trading signals.
pricing.comparisonIntro: See each plan's dataset scope and history depth at a glance. Full dataset list in the <catalog>dataset catalog</catalog>; each dataset's actual coverage and update status follow <coverage>docs coverage</coverage> (we don't fabricate per-cell coverage numbers).
pricing.comparisonFootnote: Note: TPEx daily history depth is still deferred; adjusted prices, a survivorship-safe universe, and a backtest-grade full-market baseline are not within our current publicly-claimable scope.

rows.financialStatements: Financial statements (income / balance sheet / cash flow)

about.title: Trust & source policy
about.description: Source principles and traceable design for the Taiwan-stock data platform.

trustModel.heading: Trust model
trustModel.body: Layered as canonical, fallback, and helper to avoid mixing sources; responses retain lineage and freshness.

whyUs.heading: Why this product
whyUs.body: For research, quant, and development workflows, what matters isn't just getting the data — it's that the data is traceable, verifiable, and continuously deliverable.

product.title: TWSE-first verified data API product
product.lead: The current public positioning is a TWSE-first verified baseline; TPEx historical coverage and the full-market claim remain managed as deferred / blocked.

gettingStarted.heading: Getting started
gettingStarted.body: Start with the Quickstart and the docs, then integrate from a verified baseline dataset. Treat each dataset's coverage/status notes as the source of truth for any data claim.

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.intro: This policy explains the refund principles for TW Market Data subscriptions and digital services, and serves as important reference before subscribing and paying.
refund.section1Body1: This service is a digital-content and API subscription service; once activated, you can access the platform features and data.
refund.section1Body2: Because digital services are delivered instantly, the return model for ordinary physical goods does not, as a rule, apply.
refund.section2Body1: Except as otherwise provided in this policy, subscription fees or usage fees already collected are, as a rule, non-refundable.
refund.section2Body2: If you do not wish to renew, please cancel your subscription before the next billing cycle.
refund.section3Body1: If a major platform system error renders a paid service unusable within a reasonable period, you may submit a refund request.
refund.section3Body2: We will review each case on its actual circumstances and may provide a full or partial refund, or equivalent-value service compensation.
refund.section3Body3: Whether an exceptional refund condition is met is determined by our review results and system records.
refund.section4Body1: You may cancel your subscription at any time through your account or the subscription management flow.
refund.section5Body1: To request a refund or ask about billing questions, please contact us via the contact page.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.lead1: These Terms of Use govern the rights, obligations, scope of license, and limitations of liability between you and the platform operator when you access and use TW Market Data. Please read these Terms in full before using the Service.
terms.lead2: When you register an account, create an API key, access the Service, call the Service API, or otherwise use the Service, you represent that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms; if you do not agree to all or part of these Terms, please stop using the Service.

scope.title: 1. Scope
scope.p1: These Terms apply to your use of the platform website, datasets, API, documentation, console, downloadable content, and related features.
scope.p2: The platform may separately publish supplementary rules for specific features, plans, promotions, beta features, or enterprise partnerships; once published, such supplementary rules form part of these Terms.

definitions.title: 2. Definitions
definitions.p1: Unless the context otherwise requires, the terms used in these Terms are defined as follows.
definitions.b1: Account: the member identity created by a user in order to use the Service.
definitions.b2: API Key: the access credential used to authenticate a user's calls to the Service API.
definitions.b3: Plan: the service tier set by the platform for different data scopes, quotas, features, and license conditions.
definitions.b4: Dataset: a collection of market, company, financial, event, classification, quantitative, or other data made available by the platform for query, download, analysis, or integration.
definitions.b5: Commercial Use: use for profit, providing paid services to third parties, integration into commercialized products, enterprise operational analysis, customer service, internal commercial decision support, or any other use scenario with a commercial purpose.
definitions.b6: Third-Party Data: data, content, or information not originally produced by the platform but obtained from public sources, official sources, or other third-party providers.

service.title: 3. Service Content
service.p1: The platform provides a Taiwan stock data API, dataset access capabilities, a console, documentation, and related product interfaces.
service.p2: The datasets, features, access boundaries, quotas, rate limits, license scope, and other conditions of use actually available under the Service are governed by your current account plan, the product interface, the subscription page, or any individual agreement.
service.p3: The platform may, based on product development, source availability, regulatory requirements, technical maintenance, or other reasonable factors, add, adjust, suspend, remove, or replace certain datasets, fields, features, quotas, rate limits, or product content, and may notify users by website announcement, console notice, email, or other reasonable means.

account.title: 4. Account and API Key

account.p2: You are solely responsible for all activity and requests made under your account and API key.
account.p3: If you discover that your account or API key is suspected of being leaked, subject to unauthorized access, used abnormally, or presents any other security concern, you shall immediately notify the platform and take necessary measures to disable, reset, or rotate it.
account.p4: The platform may, based on information security, abuse prevention, system stability, regulatory compliance, or reasonable risk control needs, suspend, restrict, revoke, reset, or require replacement of a specific account or API key.

license.title: 5. License Scope and Use Restrictions

Different data sources may be affected by their original license conditions, availability, update frequency, technical limitations, latency, corrections, and interruptions.

After account termination, certain data, records, logs, billing information, or content subject to statutory retention may still be retained in accordance with law, compliance, or operational needs.

privacy.title: 12. Privacy and Data Protection

privacy.p2: Please read the Privacy Policy carefully before use to understand how the platform processes data and your rights.

serviceChanges.title: 13. Service Changes, Maintenance, and Beta Features
serviceChanges.p1: The platform may change part or all of the Service based on maintenance, upgrade, security, performance, testing, correction, or architectural adjustment needs.
serviceChanges.p2: During maintenance, updates, testing, or troubleshooting, the Service may be briefly interrupted, delayed, restricted, or render certain features unavailable.
serviceChanges.p3: The platform will make reasonable efforts to minimize the impact on users, but does not guarantee that the Service will be free from interruption or anomaly at all times.

liability.title: 14. Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: To the extent permitted by applicable law, the platform shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from use, inability to use, data delay, data error, data omission, service interruption, feature restriction, Third-Party Data anomaly, or other circumstances related to the Service.

governingLaw.title: 15. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
governingLaw.p1: The interpretation, validity, and application of these Terms shall be governed by the laws of the Republic of China (Taiwan).
governingLaw.p2: For any dispute arising from or in connection with these Terms or the Service, the parties shall first seek to resolve it through good-faith negotiation; if negotiation fails, then unless otherwise mandatorily required by law, the parties agree that the Taiwan Taipei District Court shall be the court of first instance with jurisdiction.

share.protect: To protect the rights, safety, or legitimate interests of this platform, its users, or third parties.

retention.title: 4. Data Retention and Security

However, no method of transmission or storage over the internet can guarantee absolute security, and you should still properly manage your account and API keys.

cookies.title: 5. Cookies and Similar Technologies

If you wish to exercise these rights, please contact us through the platform's contact page, and we will process your request within a reasonable period.

analytics.title: Analytics & Cookies (Product Analytics)

analytics.userChoice: Users can choose to decline or allow analytics cookies in the cookie prompt.

compare.h1: How to choose a Taiwan stock data API: FinMind vs TEJ vs TW Market Data
compare.intro: Taiwan stock data APIs come in three common flavors, matching different budgets and levels of granularity. This is an honest comparison to help you pick the right tool for your scenario.

positioning.finmind: FinMind — large free tier, strong community ecosystem, deep chip-flow data. Good for getting started, personal research, and quick prototypes.
positioning.tej: TEJ (Taiwan Economic Journal) — institutional-grade, extremely clean data, complete point-in-time, includes delisted stocks. Taiwan's "Bloomberg/Compustat", but expensive with a high barrier for individual developers.
positioning.twmd: TW Market Data — first-party official value-by-value traceability, knowledge_date on every record (PIT), honest data_gaps, no-key instant trial, affordable. Positioned between FinMind's accessibility and TEJ's rigor.

compare.tableCaption: The table marks each provider's public positioning only; refer to each provider's own site for actual fields and plans. The TW Market Data column lists shipped capabilities only, with coverage transparently marked per dataset.

delisted.twmd: ✓ ({stoppedStocks} tickers of price history, back to {startYear})

scenarios.finmind: Getting started / personal research / free exploration → FinMind.
scenarios.tej: Institutional-grade, ample budget, want the most complete PIT and delisted stocks → TEJ.
scenarios.twmd: Want official traceability + point-in-time, but TEJ is too expensive / unfriendly to individuals, and you also want to feed an AI agent → TW Market Data (try 2330 with no key before deciding).

compare.traceabilityBody: Quantitative backtesting fears look-ahead bias and survivorship bias most. The former needs point-in-time — knowing what had been published "at the time"; the latter needs data that includes delisted stocks. TW Market Data carries knowledge_date on every record to support as-of replay, and retains price history for {stoppedStocks} stocks that have stopped trading, covering both biases; gaps are shown explicitly via data_gaps rather than implicitly backfilled.

otherApis.question: What Taiwan stock data APIs are there besides FinMind?
otherApis.answer: Common choices include TEJ (institutional-grade PIT), TW Market Data (official reconciliation + PIT + affordable + AI-agent), Fugle/Shioaji (broker real-time + order placement), and TWSE/TPEx OpenAPI (official, free).

bestForPit.question: Which Taiwan stock API is best for point-in-time backtesting?
bestForPit.answer: TEJ offers the most complete institutional-grade PIT; for affordable PIT that is friendly to individuals and AI agents, TW Market Data carries knowledge_date on every record, includes delisted stocks, and is honest about data_gaps.

vsFinmind.question: How does TW Market Data differ from FinMind?
vsFinmind.answer: FinMind is free with a strong community ecosystem; TW Market Data emphasizes official value-by-value traceability, a point-in-time contract, and honest data_gaps disclosure, and gives AI agents llms.txt/openapi discovery entry points. The two can be used together.

compare.relatedAiAgents: Taiwan Stock Data API for AI Agents

connect.intro: Pick a tier to start pulling first-hand official Taiwan market data — try it free, integrate as a developer, or use it inside paid chat.
connect.tier1Tickers: Any agent can pull real data instantly with the five key-free tickers (2330 TSMC / 2317 Hon Hai / 2454 MediaTek / 0050 Yuanta Taiwan 50 / 2603 Evergreen).
connect.tier2Body: Claude Code / Cursor / your own agent: get an API key from your account page, call the REST API, and pay by usage.
connect.tier2KeyWarning: Open a dedicated key for each agent (see key safety), and never paste your primary key into a conversation.
connect.tier3Body: The only supported way to pay for access inside Claude.ai / ChatGPT, and the most reliable path for tool calls: add the TW Market Data connector, authorize, and go.
connect.tier3Note: If you paste the one-liner in Claude.ai but can't get paid data, the product isn't broken — consumer chat goes through this connector tier.
connect.linkWhichTier: Which tier is right for me
connect.summary: In short: free trial goes through the one-liner, developer integration goes through an API key, paid consumer chat goes through the connector.

connectKeySafety.title: Build a dedicated key for your AI

oneKeyPerAgent.desc: Add a new key under "API Keys" in your account page and name it (e.g. claude-desktop, research-bot) so it's easy to identify later.

independentRevoke.desc: If a key leaks or is no longer used, revoke that key directly — it won't affect other agents and you never need to rotate your main key.

neverPasteMainKey.term: Never paste your main key into a conversation
neverPasteMainKey.desc: Conversation logs may be retained; give agents a dedicated sub-key only.

rotate.desc: For high-frequency or public-facing scenarios, rotate the key on a regular schedule.

connectKeySafety.capabilityNote: Capability check: the system already supports multiple named, independently revocable keys per account (<keyLabel></keyLabel> / <revokedAt></revokedAt> / <createdByCustomer></createdByCustomer>). If your account page hasn't yet enabled "add multiple keys", that's a small remaining prerequisite.

datasets.heroTitle: Taiwan stock datasets, built for APIs and research workflows
datasets.heroBody: TW Market Data organizes Taiwan-stock prices, the three financial statements, monthly revenue, and institutional net buy/sell into traceable, re-runnable, workflow-ready API products, with source lineage on every response and disclosed data_gaps preserved. The current public positioning is a TWSE-first verified baseline; TPEx historical depth and full-market claims are not yet available.
datasets.usageTitle: How stock analysis uses this data
datasets.sampleCaption: Download without registration: sample data using one of the five no-key symbols (2330 TSMC) as the example.
datasets.whyItMattersTitle: Why it matters for stock analysis
datasets.badgeGreenTitle: Every value is traceable value-by-value to the official source; this is the date of the last reconciliation that passed.
datasets.badgePendingTitle: Reconciliation is in progress; a passing date has not been produced yet.

docs.noSections: This page has no section outline yet.
docs.aiHint: Building an AI agent? Start with <llms>/llms.txt</llms> for the full site index.
docs.englishNotice: This documentation page is currently available in Chinese only; English versions are coming soon.

coverage.heading: Coverage
coverage.caption: Figures are measured from the DB; unlisted fields defer to <nowrap>docs coverage</nowrap> (coming) — we don't fabricate cell by cell.

limitations.heading: Limitations & notes
items: Coverage is not the full market or full history; the actual range is defined by the coverage table above and the data_gaps in the response.
Missing data is flagged honestly — we don't fill gaps with estimated values.
The platform focuses on daily-frequency and fundamental data; it does not provide real-time quotes, intraday bars, or cryptocurrencies.
TWSE is the verified baseline; TPEx history depth and adjusted prices are currently beta / deferred, flagged per dataset.

landing.intro1: TW Market Data turns official data from TWSE, TPEx, MOPS, and TAIFEX into a consistent, easy-to-integrate REST API. We ingest and parse every dataset directly from the official sources — never second-hand.
landing.intro2: Every response carries its source and knowledge_date; gaps are marked honestly, and holes are never filled with estimates.

access.heading: What you can access
access.intro: Daily prices for individual TWSE-listed stocks go back to <y>{year}</y>, and we keep the full price history of <c>{count}</c> stocks that have stopped trading (delisted / long-term suspended), so backtests aren't inflated by survivorship bias. Currently available:
access.betaNote: TPEx daily bars and adjusted prices are currently in beta, with coverage marked per dataset.

builtFor.heading: Who it's for
builtFor.b1: AI financial agents and LLM pipelines that need data to feed on
builtFor.b4: The internal data layer of fintech products

step0.heading: Step 0 · Try one call with zero sign-up (no key)
step0.intro: These 5 symbols work without a key: 2330 TSMC / 2317 Hon Hai / 2454 MediaTek / 0050 / 2603. Paste this to your AI or run curl directly:
step0.note: (Other stocks / other datasets need the API key below.)

getKey.heading: 1. Get an API key
getKey.body: Register in the <dash>dashboard</dash> and create an API key there. Put the key in the request header, not in the URL.

firstRequest.heading: 2. Make your first request
firstRequest.body: Every data endpoint is <endpoint></endpoint>, with the key passed in the <key></key> header.

seeResponse.heading: 3. Read the response
seeResponse.body: You get typed JSON, each record carrying its source; missing data is left empty rather than filled in. Field definitions are on each <link>dataset page</link>.

auth.intro: Every request carries the header. Create, rotate, and revoke keys yourself in the dashboard — no need to contact us.

habit1.term: Keep the key in the header, not the URL
habit1.desc: So it doesn't end up in server logs or browsing history.

habit2.desc: Keep development and production separate, so you revoke only the leaked one (multiple keys are available by plan).

habit3.desc: Each key's per-minute requests and monthly usage are metered by plan.

auth.error1: — API key missing or invalid (except the five no-key symbols 2330/2317/2454/0050/2603, which return 200 directly).
auth.error2: — your plan is not entitled to this dataset (paywall).

I'd like to delete my TW Market Data account.

done.body: Your account and API keys have been deleted; the keys are now invalid.
done.localNotDeleted: Your API account was deleted, but your website login could not be removed at the same time. Contact {email} and we'll clear it manually.

confirm.body: This action cannot be undone. Your account and API keys will be deleted; the keys stop working immediately and can no longer call the API.
confirm.retentionNote: Transaction and invoice records that tax and accounting rules require us to keep will be listed for you after deletion.
confirm.fallback: Self-service deletion isn't available yet. Tap the button below to submit a deletion request; we'll process it manually and confirm by email.

current.overageNote: Once the included quota is used up, excess requests are billed from the credits wallet (overage); RPM is the per-minute rate limit, counted separately from quota and credits.

invoices.unavailable: Billing history is temporarily unavailable. Please try again shortly.

balance.title: Balance
balance.subtitle: Available credits balance
balance.zeroNote: You have no credits available yet — a balance of 0 is normal; it will appear after you top up or subscribe.

costTable.title: API endpoints
costTable.subtitle: Credit cost per API call
costTable.note: Credit usage depends on endpoint cost and request volume (currently showing: {mode}).

billingMethod.title: How billing works
billingMethod.b1: Subscription plans include a monthly request quota; API requests draw from this quota first, leaving the credits wallet untouched.
billingMethod.b2: Once the included quota is used up, excess requests are charged from the credits wallet at the endpoint costs above (overage).
billingMethod.b3: When the included quota is exhausted and the wallet balance is insufficient, requests are rejected (top up or upgrade your plan).
billingMethod.b4: RPM (requests per minute) is a rate safeguard, counted separately from the monthly quota and credits.
billingMethod.dryRunNote: Billing is in dry-run mode: requests record an estimated cost but are not yet charged to credits or your card.
billingMethod.enabledNote: Billing is active: requests beyond the included quota are charged at the endpoint costs.

reconciliation.title: Usage / credits reconciliation (last {days} days)
reconciliation.detail: Usage events: {events} · Estimated usage cost (incl. dry-run): {estimated} · Credits actually charged: {charged} · Wallet balance: {balance}
reconciliation.dryRunHint: Currently in dry-run mode; some requests have not yet entered the live charging flow.
reconciliation.partial: Some requests are not yet reconciled
reconciliation.mismatch: Needs manual review: {mismatched} amount mismatches · {duplicate} duplicate charges · {orphan} charges with no matching request

topup.note: After payment, Polar notifies us and credits are added to your wallet automatically; if they don’t appear yet, refresh in a moment.

aiResearch.title: AI Research
aiResearch.intro: Generate auditable research conclusions, risk checks, and simulated decisions from a Taiwan-stock data workflow.
aiResearch.planAvailability: Available on the Pro, Team, and Enterprise plans; Developer provides mock preview only.

local.label: Local mock
local.description: Uses the browser's built-in deterministic mock response; the backend is not called.

proxy.label: tw-ai mock proxy
proxy.description: Calls the tw-ai mock endpoint through the local internal proxy.

fallback.label: Proxy unavailable; reverted to local mock
fallback.description: The backend mock proxy is temporarily unavailable; the page has fallen back to the local deterministic mock.

aiResearch.noCredits: This is a local mock entitlement; no credits are deducted.
aiResearch.englishNotice: The research output below is currently available in Chinese only; English versions are coming soon.

availabilityHint.ready: TWSE price data for the past 6 months is available, allowing the Market Data Analyst to enter mock-real.
availabilityHint.unavailable: No price data found for this ticker; the Market Data Analyst will be marked as missing.
availabilityHint.betaLimited: TPEx historical depth is not yet complete, so the research workflow can only fall back conservatively.
availabilityHint.degraded: A data-coverage limitation was detected; the research workflow degrades conservatively and stays at no_action.

aiResearch.technicalFixtureNote: The technical view is currently a deterministic fixture, shown only for the mock research workflow; it does not represent full live technical analysis.

order.title: Simulated order
order.note: This is simulated research output; no broker order is ever created.

entitlementHelper.free: AI Research is not available on this plan.
entitlementHelper.starter: Mock preview only — suitable for development and validation workflows.
entitlementHelper.pro: Provides a single-ticker basic research workflow with a decision log.
entitlementHelper.max: Includes batch research and simulated portfolio (this round shows UI only, no functional implementation).
entitlementHelper.developer: Highest self-serve research quota, including batch and simulated portfolio (this round shows UI only).
entitlementHelper.enterprise: Supports planning custom workflows and custom quotas (this round shows UI only, no functional implementation).

entitlementUpgrade.free: AI Research is a paid feature. Upgrade to Starter or above to use it.

terminal.title: Research Terminal
terminal.description: An online research workbench built on official Taiwan-stock data: factors, data review, and research workflows in one place.
terminal.descriptionEnabled: Once signed in, you can jump straight here without signing in again.
